votes.reason: The Python code correctly simplifies the Coq specification, as a number can be written as the sum of 4 positive even numbers if and only if it is even and at least 8.
The Python code correctly simplifies the existence of four positive even numbers summing to n into the equivalent mathematical condition n >= 8 and n % 2 == 0.
The Python code correctly simplifies the Coq specification by checking if the number is even and at least 8, which is mathematically equivalent to being the sum of 4 positive even numbers.

The Coq spec states b=true iff n can be written as sum of 4 positive even numbers. The smallest positive even is 2, so minimum sum is 8. Any even n>=8 can be written as 2+2+2+(n-6) where n-6 is positive even when n>=8 and n is even. The Python spec correctly captures this as n>=8 and n%2==0, which matches the Coq specification's concrete behavior.

Both characterize that n is a sum of four positive even numbers exactly when n is even and at least 8; helper and spec match on all integers.
Sum of four positive evens exists iff n is even and at least 8; Python encodes this criterion and the helper matches the Coq definition of positive even.
Both define positive even numbers identically and characterize sums of four positive evens as exactly the even integers >= 8; Python returns b == (n >= 8 and n % 2 == 0), matching the Coq spec.
